text.KJV: 11:38 Heare thou, I will talke with thee, and the highest shall say vnto thee,

text.DRB: Is it not thou that hast overcome of the four beasts, which I made to reign in my world, and that by them the end of their times might come?
text.KJV: 11:39 Art not thou it that remainest of the foure beasts, whom I made to raigne in my world, that the end of their times might come through them?

11:40 And the fourth came and ouercame all the beasts that were past, and had power ouer the world with great fearefulnesse, and ouer the whole compasse of the earth with much wicked oppression, and so long time dwelt he vpon the earth with deceit.
text.NRSV: “The fourth has come and has conquered all the creatures that have been before; and has kept the world in his control with much oppression.”
text.DRB: And the fourth coming overcame all the beasts that were past, and by might held the world with much fear, and all the world with most wicked labour, and he inhabited the whole earth so long time with deceit.
And thou hast judged the earth not with truth.
text.KJV: 11:41 For the earth hast thou not iudged with trueth.

text.DRB: For thou hast afflicted the meek, and hast troubled them that were quiet, and hast loved liars, and hast destroyed their habitations that did fructify, & hast overthrown their walls that did not hurt thee.
text.KJV: 11:42 For thou hast afflicted the meeke, thou hast hurt the peaceable, thou hast loued lyers, and destroyed the dwellings of them that brought forth fruite, and hast cast downe the walles of such, as did thee no harme.

text.DRB: And thy contumely is ascended even to the Highest, and thy pride to the Strong.
text.KJV: 11:43 Therefore is thy wrongfull dealing come vp vnto the Highest, and thy pride vnto the Mighty.

text.DRB: And the Highest hath looked upon the proud times: and behold they are ended, and the abominations thereof are accomplished.
text.KJV: 11:44 The Highest also hath looked vpon the proud times, and behold, they are ended, and his abominations are fulfilled.

text.DRB: Therefore thou eagle appear no more, and thy horrible wings and thy little wings most wicked, and thy heads malignant, and thy talons most wicked, and all thy body vain,
text.KJV: 11:45 And therefore appeare no more thou Eagle, nor thy horrible wings, nor thy wicked feathers, nor thy malitious heads, nor thy hurtfull clawes, nor all thy vaine body:

text.DRB: that all the earth may be refreshed, and may return delivered from thy violence, and may hope for his judgement and mercy that made it.
text.KJV: 11:46 That all the earth may be refreshed, and may returne, being deliuered from thy violence, and that she may hope for the iudgement, and mercy of him that made her.
12:1 And it came to passe whiles the Lyon spake these words vnto the Eagle, I saw:
text.NRSV: While the lion was saying these words to the eagle, I looked,

text.DRB: and behold the head that had overcome, and those four wings appeared not which passed to him, and were set up to reign: and their reign was small, and full of tumult.
text.KJV: 12:2 And behold, the head that remained, and the foure wings appeared no more, and the two went vnto it, and set themselues vp to raigne, and their kingdome was small and full of vprore.

12:3 And I saw, and behold, they appeared no more, and the whole body of the Eagle was burnt, so that the earth was in great feare: then awaked I out of the trouble and traunce of my minde, and from great feare, and said vnto my spirit,
text.NRSV: and behold, the head that remained and the four wings disappeared, and the whole body of the eagle was burned, and the earth was exceedingly terrified.
text.DRB: And I saw, and behold they appeared not, & all the body of the eagle was burnt, and the earth was afraid exceedingly, & I by the tumult & trance of mind, and for great fear awaked, and said to my spirit:
Behold thou hast given me this, in that, that thou searchest the ways of the Highest.
text.KJV: 12:4 Loe, this hast thou done vnto me, in that thou searchest out the wayes of the Highest.

text.DRB: Behold yet I am weary in mind, and in my spirit I am very feeble, and there is not so much as a little strength in me for the great fear, that I was afraid of this night.
text.KJV: 12:5 Loe, yet am I weary in my mind, and very weake in my spirit: and litle strength is there in me; for the great feare, wherewith I was affrighted this night.

text.DRB: Now therefore I will pray the Highest, that he strengthen me even to the end.
text.KJV: 12:6 Therefore wil I now beseech the Highest, that hee will comfort me vnto the end.

text.DRB: And I said: Lord Dominatour, if I have found grace before thine eyes, and if I am justified before thee above many, and if indeed my prayer be ascended before thy face,
text.KJV: 12:7 And I said, Lord, that bearest rule, If I haue found grace before thy sight, and if I am iustified with thee, before many others, and if my prayer indeed be come vp before thy face,

text.DRB: Strengthen me, and shew unto me thy servant the interpretation, and distinction of this horrible vision, that thou mayst comfort my soul most fully.
text.KJV: 12:8 Comfort me then, and shew me thy seruant the interpretation, and plaine difference of this fearefull vision, that thou maist perfectly comfort my soule.

text.DRB: For thou hast counted me worthy to shew unto me the later times. And he said to me:
text.KJV: 12:9 For thou hast iudged me worthy, to shew me the last times.

text.DRB: This is the interpretation of this vision.
text.KJV: 12:10 And he said vnto me, This is the interpretation of the vision.

text.DRB: The eagle which thou sawest coming up from the sea, this is the Kingdom which was seen in a vision to Daniel thy brother.
text.KJV: 12:11 The Eagle whom thou sawest come vp from the sea, is the kingdome which was seene, in the vision of thy brother Daniel.

text.DRB: But it was not interpreted to him, therefore I do now interpret it to thee.
text.KJV: 12:12 But it was not expounded vnto him, therefore now I declare it vnto thee.

text.DRB: Behold the days come, and there shall rise a Kingdom upon the earth, and the fear shall be more terrible than of all the Kingdoms that were before it.
text.KJV: 12:13 Behold, the dayes will come, that there shall rise vp a kingdome vpon earth, and it shall be feared aboue all the kingdomes that were before it.

text.DRB: And there shall twelve kings reign in it, one after another.
text.KJV: 12:14 In the same shall twelue kings reigne, one after another.

text.DRB: For the second shall begin to reign, and he shall continue more time than the rest of the twelve.
text.KJV: 12:15 Whereof the second shall begin to reigne, and shall haue more time then any of the twelue.

text.DRB: And the voice that spake which thou heardest, not coming forth of her heads, but from the midst of her body,
text.KJV: 12:17 As for the voice which thou heardest speake, and that thou sawest not to goe out from the heads, but from the mids of the body thereof, this is the interpretation:

text.DRB: this is the interpretation, that after the time of that Kingdom shall rise no small contentions, and it shall be in danger to fall: and it shall not fall then, but shall be constituted again according to the beginning thereof.
text.KJV: 12:18 That after the time of that kingdome, there shall arise great striuings, and it shall stand in perill of falling: neuerthelesse it shall not then fall, but shal be restored againe to his beginning.

text.DRB: And whereas thou sawest eight underwings cleaving to the wings thereof,
text.KJV: 12:19 And whereas thou sawest the eight small vnder feathers sticking to her wings, this is the interpretation:

text.DRB: this is the interpretation: eight kings shall arise in it, whose times shall be light, and years swift, and two of them shall perish.
text.KJV: 12:20 That in him there shal arise eight kings, whose time shall bee but small, and their yeeres swift.

text.DRB: But when the middest time approacheth, four shall be kept till a time, when the time thereof shall begin to approach to be ended, yet two shall be kept to the end.
text.KJV: 12:21 And two of them shall perish: the middle time approching, foure shall bee kept vntill their end begin to approch: but two shall be kept vnto the end.

text.DRB: And whereas thou sawest three heads resting,
text.KJV: 12:22 And whereas thou sawest three heads resting, this is the interpretation

text.DRB: this is the interpretation: in her last days the Highest will raise up three Kingdoms, and will call back many things into them, and they shall rule over the earth,
text.KJV: 12:23 In his last dayes shall the most High raise vp three kingdomes, and renew many things therein, and they shal haue the dominion of the earth,

text.DRB: and them that dwell in it, with much labour above all them that were before them. For this cause they are called the heads of the eagle.
text.KJV: 12:24 And of those that dwell therein with much oppression, aboue all those that were before them: therefore are they called the heads of the Eagle.

text.DRB: For these shall be they that shall recapitulate her impieties, and that shall accomplish her last things.
text.KJV: 12:25 For these are they that shal accomplish his wickednesse, and that shall finish his last end.

text.DRB: And whereas thou sawest a greater head not appearing, this is the interpretation thereof: that one of them shall die upon his bed, and yet with torments.
text.KJV: 12:26 And whereas thou sawest that the great head appeared no more, it signifieth that one of them shall die vpon his bed, and yet with paine.

text.DRB: For the two that shall remain, the sword shall eat them.
text.KJV: 12:27 For the two that remaine, shall be slaine with the sword.

text.DRB: For the sword of one shall devour him that is with him: but yet this also at the last shall fall by the sword.
text.KJV: 12:28 For the sword of the one shall deuoure the other: but at the last shall he fall through the sword himselfe.

text.DRB: And whereas thou sawest two underwings passing over the head that is on the right side,
text.KJV: 12:29 And whereas thou sawest two feathers vnder the wings passing ouer the head, that is on the right side:

text.DRB: this is the interpretation: these are they whom the Highest hath kept to their end, this is a small Kingdom, and full of trouble.
text.KJV: 12:30 It signifieth that these are they whom the Highest hath kept vnto their end: this is the small kingdom and full of trouble, as thou sawest.
12:31 And the Lyon whom thou sawest rising vp out of the wood, and roaring, and speaking to the Eagle, and rebuking her for her vnrighteousnesse, with all the words which thou hast heard,
text.NRSV: “As for the lion whom you saw rousing up out of the forest and roaring and speaking to the eagle... he is the Messiah whom the Most High has kept until the end of days.”
text.DRB: As thou sawest the lion also, whom thou sawest awaking out of the wood, and roaring, and speaking to the eagle, and rebuking her, and her injustices by all his words as thou hast heard:
this is the wind which the Highest hath kept unto the end for them, and their impieties: and he shall rebuke them, and shall cast in their spoils before them.
text.KJV: 12:32 This is the Anointed which the Highest hath kept for them, and for their wickednesse vnto the end: he shall reprooue them, and shall vpbraid them with their crueltie.

text.DRB: For he shall set them in judgement alive: and it shall be, when he hath reproved them, then shall he chastise them.
text.KJV: 12:33 For hee shall set them before him aliue in iudgement, and shall rebuke them and correct them.

text.DRB: For the rest of my people he shall deliver with misery, them that are saved upon my borders, and he shall make them joyful till the end shall come, the day of judgement, whereof I have spoken to thee from the beginning.
text.KJV: 12:34 For the rest of my people shall he deliuer with mercie, those that haue bin preserued vpon my borders, and he shal make them ioyfull vntill the comming of the day of iudgement, whereof I haue spoken vnto thee from the beginning.

text.DRB: This is the dream which thou sawest, and these be the interpretations.
text.KJV: 12:35 This is the dreame that thou sawest, and these are the interpretations.

text.DRB: Thou therefore only hast been worthy to know this secret of the Highest.
text.KJV: 12:36 Thou onely hast bene meete to know this secret of the Highest.

text.DRB: Write therefore in a Book all these things which thou hast seen, and put them in a hidden place:
text.KJV: 12:37 Therefore write all these things that thou hast seene, in a booke, and hide them.

text.DRB: and thou shalt teach them the wise men of thy people, whose hearts thou knowest able to take, & to keep these secrets.
text.KJV: 12:38 And teach them to the wise of the people, whose hearts thou knowest may comprehend, & keepe these seerets.

text.DRB: But do thou stay here yet other seven days, that there may be shewed thee whatsoever shall seem good to the Highest to shew thee.
text.KJV: 12:39 But wait thou here thy selfe yet seuen dayes moe, that it may be shewed thee whatsoeuer it pleaseth the Highest to declare vnto thee: And with that he went his way.

text.DRB: And he departed from me, And it came to pass, when all the people had heard that the seven days were past, and I had not returned into the city, and all gathered themselves together from the least unto the greatest: and came to me, and spake to me saying:
text.KJV: 12:40 And it came to passe when all the people saw that the seuen dayes were past, and I not come againe into the citie, they gathered them all together, from the least vnto the greatest, and came vnto me, and said,

text.DRB: What have we sinned to thee, or what have we done unjustly against thee, that leaving us thou hast sitten in this place?
text.KJV: 12:41 What haue we offended thee? and what euill haue we done against thee, that thou forsakest vs, and sittest here in this place?

text.DRB: For thou alone art remaining to us of all peoples, as a cluster of grapes of the vineyard, and as a candle in a dark place, and as an haven and ship saved from the tempest.
text.KJV: 12:42 For of all the prophets thou only art left vs, as a cluster of the vintage, and as a candle in a darke place, and as a hauen or ship preserued from the tempest:

text.DRB: Or are not the evils that chance, sufficient for us?
text.KJV: 12:43 Are not the euils which are come to vs, sufficient?

text.DRB: If then thou shalt forsake us, how much better had it been to us, if we also had been burnt with the burning of Sion?
text.KJV: 12:44 If thou shalt forsake vs, how much better had it bene for vs, if we also had bene burnt in the midst of Sion.

text.DRB: For we are not better than they that died there. And they wept with a loud voice. And I answered them, & said:
text.KJV: 12:45 For we are not better then they that died there. And they wept with a loud voice: then answered I them, and said,

text.DRB: Be of good cheer Israel, & be not sorrowful thou house of Jacob.
text.KJV: 12:46 Be of good comfort, O Israel, and be not heauy thou house of Iacob.

text.DRB: For there is remembrance of you before the Highest, and the Strong hath not forgotten you in tentation.
text.KJV: 12:47 For the Highest hath you in remembrance, and the mighty hath not forgotten you in temptation.

text.DRB: For I have not forsaken you, neither did I depart from you: but I came into this place, to pray for the desolation of Sion, and to seek mercy for the low estate of your sanctification.
text.KJV: 12:48 As for mee, I haue not forsaken you, neither am I departed from you: but am come into this place, to pray for the desolation of Sion, and that I might seeke mercy for the low estate of your Sanctuary.

text.DRB: And now go every one of you into his house, and I will come to you after these days.
text.KJV: 12:49 And now goe your way home euery man, and after these dayes will I come vnto you.

text.DRB: And the people departed, as I said to them, into the city:
text.KJV: 12:50 So the people went their way into the city, like as I commanded them:

text.DRB: but I sat in the field seven days, as he commanded me: and I did eat of the flowers of the field only, of the herbs was my meat made in those
text.KJV: 12:51 But I remained still in the field seuen dayes, as the Angel commanded me, and did eate onely in those dayes, of the flowers of the fielde, and had my meat of the herbes.
13:1 And it came to passe after seuen dayes, I dreamed a dreame by night.
text.NRSV: After seven days I dreamed a dream in the night.

text.KJV: 13:2 And loe, there arose a winde from the sea that it mooued all the waues thereof.
text.NRSV: And behold, a wind arose from the sea and stirred up all its waves.
text.DRB: And behold there rose a wind from the sea, that troubled all the waves thereof.

text.KJV: 13:3 And I beheld, and loe, that man waxed strong with the thousands of heauen: and when he turned his countenance to looke, all the things trembled that were seene vnder him.
text.NRSV: I looked, and behold, this wind brought up out of the midst of the sea something like the figure of a man; and I looked, and behold, that man flew with the clouds of heaven.
text.DRB: And I saw, and behold that man grew strong with thousands of heaven: and when he turned his countenance to consider, all things trembled that were seen under him:
& wheresoever voice proceeded out of his mouth, all that heard his voices began to burn, as the earth is quiet when it feeleth the fire.
text.KJV: 13:4 And whensoeuer the voyce went out of his mouth, all they burnt, that heard his voyce, like as the earth faileth when it feeleth the fire.

text.DRB: And I saw after these, and behold a multitude of men was gathered together, of whom there was no number, from the four winds of heaven, to fight against the man that was come up out of the sea.
text.KJV: 13:5 And after this I beheld, and loe, there was gathered together a multitude of men out of number, from the foure windes of the heauen, to subdue the man that came out of the sea.

text.DRB: And I saw, and behold he had graven to himself a great mountain, and he flew upon it.
text.KJV: 13:6 But I beheld, and loe, hee had graued himselfe a great mountaine, and flew vp vpon it.

text.DRB: And I sought to see the country, or the place whence the mountain was graven, and I could not.
text.KJV: 13:7 But I would haue seene the region, or place, whereout the hill was grauen, and I could not.

text.DRB: And after these things I saw, and behold all that were gathered to him, to overthrow him, feared exceedingly, yet they were bold to fight.
text.KJV: 13:8 And after this I beheld, and loe, all they which were gathered together to subdue him, were sore afraid, and yet durst fight.

text.DRB: And behold as he saw the violence of the multitude that came, he lifted not up his hand, nor held sword, nor any warlike instrument, but only as I saw,
text.KJV: 13:9 And loe, as hee saw the violence of the multitude that came, hee neither lift vp his hand, nor held sword, nor any instrument of warre.

text.DRB: that he sent forth out of his mouth as it were a blast of fire, and from his lips a spirit of flame, & from his tongue he sent forth sparkles and tempests, & all things were mingled together with this blast of fire, and spirit of flame, and multitude of tempests.
text.KJV: 13:10 But onely I saw that he sent out of his mouth, as it had bene a blast of fire, and out of his lippes a flaming breath, and out of his tongue he cast out sparkes and tempests,

text.DRB: And it fell with violence upon the multitude, that was prepared to fight, and burned them all, that suddenly there was nothing seen of an innumerable multitude, but only dust, and the savour of smoke: and I saw, and was afraid.
text.KJV: 13:11 And they were all mixt together; the blast of fire, the flaming breath, and the great tempest, and fel with violence vpon the multitude, which was prepared to fight, and burnt them vp euery one, so that vpon a sudden, of an innumerable multitude, nothing was to be perceiued, but onely dust and smell of smoke: whē I saw this, I was afraid.

text.DRB: And after these things I saw the man himself descending from the mountain, and calling to him another peaceable multitude,
text.KJV: 13:12 Afterward saw I the same man come downe from the mountaine, and call vnto him an other peaceable multitude.

text.DRB: and there came to him the countenance of many men, some rejoicing, and some sorrowing: and some bond, some bringing of them that were offered. And I was sick for much fear, & awaked, and said.
text.KJV: 13:13 And there came much people vnto him, whereof some were glad, some were sory, some of them were bound, and other some brought of them that were offred: then was I sicke through great feare, and I awaked and said,

text.DRB: Thou from the beginning hast shewed thy servant these marvelous things, and hast counted me worthy that thou wouldest receive my petition.
text.KJV: 13:14 Thou hast shewed thy seruant wonders from the beginning, and hast counted me worthy that thou shouldest receiue my prayer:

text.DRB: And now shew me yet the interpretation of this dream.
text.KJV: 13:15 Shew mee now yet the interpretation of this dreame.

text.DRB: For as I think in my judgement, woe to them that were left in those days: and much more woe to them that were not left.
text.KJV: 13:16 For as I conceiue in mine vnderstanding, woe vnto them that shall be left in those dayes; and much more woe vnto them that are not left behinde.

text.DRB: For they that were not left, were sorrowful.
text.KJV: 13:17 For they that were not left, were in heauinesse.

text.DRB: I understand now what things are laid up in the later days, and they shall happen to them, yea and to them that are left.
text.KJV: 13:18 Now vnderstand I the things that are layde vp in the latter dayes, which shall happen vnto them, and to those that are left behinde.

text.DRB: For therefore they came into great dangers, and many necessities, as these dreams do shew.
text.KJV: 13:19 Therefore are they come into great perils, and many necessities, like as these dreames declare.

text.DRB: But yet it is easier, adventuring to come into it, than to pass, as a cloud from the world, and not to see the things that happen in the later time. And he answered me, and said:
text.KJV: 13:20 Yet is it easier for him that is in danger, to come into these things, then to passe away as a cloud out of the world, and not to see the things that happen in the last dayes. And he answered vnto me, and said,

text.DRB: Both the interpretation of the vision I will tell thee: and also concerning the things that thou hast spoken I will open to thee.
text.KJV: 13:21 The interpretation of the vision shal I shew thee, and I wil open vnto thee, the thing that thou hast required.

text.DRB: Whereas thou speakest of them that were left, this is the interpretation.
text.KJV: 13:22 Wheras thou hast spoken of them that are left behinde, this is the interpretation.

text.DRB: He that taketh away danger at that time, he hath guarded himself. They that have fallen into danger, these are they that have works, and faith in the Strongest.
text.KJV: 13:23 He that shall endure the perill in that time, hath kept himselfe: they that be fallen into danger, are such as haue workes, and faith towards the Almightie.

text.DRB: Know therefore that they are more blessed which are left, than they that are dead.
text.KJV: 13:24 Know this therefore, that they which be left behinde, are more blessed then they that be dead.
13:25 This is the meaning of the vision: Whereas thou sawest a man comming vp from the middest of the Sea:
text.NRSV: “This is the interpretation of the vision: As for your seeing a man coming up from the heart of the sea,”
text.DRB: These are the interpretations of the vision, whereas thou sawest a man coming from the heart of the sea,

text.KJV: 13:26 The same is hee whom God the highest hath kept a great season, which by his owne selfe shall deliuer his creature: and hee shall order them that are left behinde.
text.NRSV: “this is he whom the Most High has been keeping for many ages, who will himself deliver his creation; and he will direct those who are left.”
text.DRB: the same is he whom the Highest preserveth much time, which by himself shall deliver his creature: and he shall dispose them that are left.
And whereas thou sawest proceed out of his mouth, as it were wind, and fire, and tempest:
text.KJV: 13:27 And whereas thou sawest, that out of his mouth there came as a blast of winde, and fire, and storme:

text.DRB: and whereas he held no sword, nor warlike instrument: for his violence destroyed the multitude that came to overthrow him: this is the interpretation.
text.KJV: 13:28 And that he helde neither sword, nor any instrument of warre, but that the rushing in of him destroyed the whole multitude that came to subdue him, this is the interpretation.

text.DRB: Behold the days come, when the Highest shall begin to deliver them, that are upon the earth:
text.KJV: 13:29 Behold, the dayes come, when the most high wil begin to deliuer them that are vpon the earth.

text.DRB: and he shall come in excess of mind upon them that inhabit the earth.
text.KJV: 13:30 And he shall come to the astonishment of them that dwell on the earth.

text.DRB: And one shall think
text.KJV: 13:31 And one shall vndertake to fight against another, one city against another, one place against another, one people against another, and one realme against another.

text.DRB: And it shall be, when these things shall come to pass, and the signs shall happen, which I shewed thee before: and then shall my son be revealed, whom thou sawest, as a man coming up.
text.KJV: 13:32 And the time shalbe, when these things shall come to passe, and the signes shall happen which I shewed thee before, and then shall my sonne be declared, whom thou sawest as a man ascending.

text.DRB: And it shall be when all nations shall hear his voice: and every one in his country shall leave their war, that they have toward each other:
text.KJV: 13:33 And when all the people heare his voice, euery man shall in their owne land, leaue the battaile they haue one against another.

text.DRB: and an innumerable multitude shall be gathered in one, as willing to come to overthrow him.
text.KJV: 13:34 And an innumerable multitude shalbe gathered together, as thou sawest them willing to come, and to ouercome him by fighting.

text.DRB: But he shall stand upon the top of mount Sion.
text.KJV: 13:35 But hee shall stand vpon the top of the mount Sion.

text.DRB: And Sion shall come, and it shall be shewed to all prepared and builded, as thou sawest the mountain to be graven without hands.
text.KJV: 13:36 And Sion shall come and shall be shewed to all men, being prepared and builded, like as thou sawest the hill grauen without hands.

text.DRB: And the same my son shall reprove the things that the Gentiles have invented, these their impieties which came near to the tempest, because of their evil cogitations, and torments wherewith they shall begin to be tormented.
text.KJV: 13:37 And this my sonne shall rebuke the wicked inuentions of those nations, which for their wicked life are fallen into the tempest,

text.DRB: Which were likened to the flame, and he shall destroy them without labour by the law that was likened to the fire.
text.KJV: 13:38 And shall lay before them their euill thoughts, and the torments wherwith they shall begin to be tormented, which are like vnto a flame: and hee shall destroy them without labour, by the law which is like vnto fire.

text.DRB: And whereas thou sawest him gathering unto him another peaceable multitude.
text.KJV: 13:39 And whereas thou sawest that hee gathered another peaceable multitude vnto him;

text.DRB: These are the ten tribes, which were made captive out of their land in the days of Osee the king, whom Salmanasar the king of the Assyrians led captive: and he transported them beyond the river, and they were transported into another land. 4. .
text.KJV: 13:40 Those are the ten tribes, which were caried away prisoners out of their owne land, in the time of Osea the king, whom Salmanasar the king of Assyria ledde away captiue, and hee caried them ouer the waters, and so came they into another land.

text.DRB: But they gave themselves this counsel, to forsake the multitude of nations, and to go forth into a farther country, where mankind never inhabited.
text.KJV: 13:41 But they tooke this counsaile amongst themselues, that they would leaue the multitude of the heathen, and goe foorth into a further countrey, where neuer mankind dwelt,

text.DRB: Or there to observe their ordinances, which they had not kept in their country.
text.KJV: 13:42 That they might there keepe their statutes, which they neuer kept in their owne land.

text.DRB: And they entered in by the narrow entrances of the river Euphrates.
text.KJV: 13:43 And they entred into Euphrates by the narrow passages of the Riuer.

text.DRB: For the Highest then wrought them signs, and stayed the veins of the river till they passed. Exod. 14. Iosue. 3.
text.KJV: 13:44 For the most high then shewed signes for them, and held still the flood, till they were passed ouer.

text.DRB: For by that country was a great way to go, of one year and a half: for the country is called Arsareth.
text.KJV: 13:45 For through that countrey there was a great way to goe; namely, of a yeere and a halfe: and the same region is called Arsareth.

text.DRB: Then did they inhabit there till in the later time: and now again when they begin to come,
text.KJV: 13:46 Then dwelt they there vntill the latter time; and now when they shall begin to come,

text.DRB: again the Highest shall stay the veins of the river, that they may pass: for these thou sawest a multitude with peace.
text.KJV: 13:47 The highest shall stay the springs of the streame againe, that they may go through: therefore sawest thou the multitude with peace.

text.DRB: But they also that were left of the people, these are they that be within my border.
text.KJV: 13:48 But those that be left behinde of thy people, are they that are found within my borders.

text.DRB: It shall come to pass therefore, when he shall begin to destroy the multitude of these nations, that are gathered, he shall protect them that have overcome the people:
text.KJV: 13:49 Now when hee destroyeth the multitude of the nations that are gathered together, he shal defend his people that remaine.

text.DRB: and then shall he shew them very many wonders.
text.KJV: 13:50 And then shall hee shewe them great wonders.

text.DRB: And I said: Lord dominatour, shew me this, why I saw a man coming up from the heart of the sea, and he said to me:
text.KJV: 13:51 Then said I, O Lord, that bearest rule, shew me this: Wherefore haue I seene the man comming vp from the midst of the Sea?

text.DRB: As thou canst not either search these things, or know what things are in the depth of the sea: so cannot any man upon the earth see my son, or them that are with him, but in the time of a day.
text.KJV: 13:52 And he said vnto me, Like as thou canst neither seeke out, nor know the things that are in the deepe of the sea: euen so can no man vpon earth see my sonne, or those that be with him, but in the day time.

text.DRB: This is the interpretation of the dream which thou sawest, and for the which thou only art here illuminated.
text.KJV: 13:53 This is the interpretation of the dreame which thou sawest, and whereby thou onely art here lightened.

text.DRB: For thou hast left thine own law, and hast been occupied about my law, and hast sought it.
text.KJV: 13:54 For thou hast forsaken thine owne way, and applied thy diligence vnto my law, and sought it.

text.DRB: For thou hast disposed thy life in wisdom, and thine understanding thou hast called mother:
text.KJV: 13:55 Thy life hast thou ordered in wisdome, and hast called vnderstanding thy mother.

text.DRB: and for this I have shewed thee riches with the Highest. For it shall be after other three days, I will speak other things to thee, and I will expound to thee weighty and marvelous things.
text.KJV: 13:56 And therefore haue I shewed thee the treasures of the Highest: After other three dayes, I will speake other things vnto thee, and declare vnto thee mightie and wonderous things.

text.DRB: And I went forth, and passed into the field, much glorifying and praising the Highest for the marvelous things that he did by time.
text.KJV: 13:57 Then went I forth into the field giuing praise and thanks greatly vnto the most High, because of his wonders which he did in time,

text.DRB: And because he governeth it, and the things that are brought in times, and I sat there three days.
text.KJV: 13:58 And because hee gouerneth the same, and such things as fall in their seasons, and there I sate three dayes.
14:1 And it came to passe, vpon the third day I sate vnder an oke, and behold, there came a voyce out of a bush ouer against me, and said, Esdras, Esdras.
text.NRSV: On the third day, while I was sitting under an oak, behold, a voice came out of a bush opposite me and said, “Ezra, Ezra.”

text.DRB: And behold a voice came forth against me out of a bush, and said: Esdras, Esdras: and I said: Lo here I am Lord. And I arose upon my feet. And he said to me:
text.KJV: 14:2 And I said, Here am I Lord, and I stood vp vpon my feet.
14:3 Then said he vnto me, In the bush I did manifestly reueale my selfe vnto Moses, and talked with him, when my people serued in Egypt.
text.NRSV: Then he said to me, “I revealed myself in a bush and spoke to Moses when my people were in bondage in Egypt;”
text.DRB: Revealing I was revealed upon the bush, and spake to Moyses, when the people served in Ægypt, Exod. 3.
and I sent him, and brought my people out of Ægypt, and brought him upon mount Sina, and held him with me many days.
text.KJV: 14:4 And I sent him, and led my people out of Egypt, and brought him vp to the mount of Sinai, where I held him by me, a long season,

text.DRB: And I told him many marvelous things, and shewed him the secrets of times, and the end: and I commanded him, saying:
text.KJV: 14:5 And told him many wonderous things, and shewed him the secrets of the times, and the end, and commanded him, saying,

text.DRB: These words thou shalt publish abroad, and these thou shalt hide.
text.KJV: 14:6 These wordes shalt thou declare, and these shalt thou hide.

text.DRB: And now to thee I say:
text.KJV: 14:7 And now I say vnto thee,

text.DRB: The signs which I have shewed, and the dreams which thou hast seen, and the interpretations which thou hast seen, lay them up in thy heart.
text.KJV: 14:8 That thou lay vp in thy heart the signes that I haue shewed, and the dreames that thou hast seene, and the interpretations which thou hast heard:

text.DRB: For thou shalt be received of all, thou shalt be converted the residue with thy counsel, and with the like to thee, till the times be finished.
text.KJV: 14:9 For thou shalt be taken away from all, and from henceforth thou shalt remaine with my sonne, and with such as be like thee, vntill the times be ended.

text.DRB: Because the world hath lost his youth, & the times draw near to wax old.
text.KJV: 14:10 For the world hath lost his youth, and the times begin to waxe old.

text.DRB: For the world is divided by twelve parts, and the tenth part, and half of the tenth part are passed:
text.KJV: 14:11 For the world is diuided into twelue parts, and the ten parts of it are gone already, and halfe of a tenth part.

text.DRB: and there remaineth hereafter the half of the tenth part.
text.KJV: 14:12 And there remaineth that which is after the halfe of the tenth part.

text.DRB: Now therefore dispose thy house, and correct thy people, & comfort the humble of them, & forsake now corruption.
text.KJV: 14:13 Now therefore set thine house in order, and reproue thy people, comfort such of them as be in trouble, and now renounce corruption.

text.DRB: And it came to pass the third day, and I sat under an oak.
text.KJV: 14:14 Let go frō thee mortall thoughts, cast away the burdens of man, put off now the weake nature,

text.DRB: for the evils which thou hast seen to have chanced now, worse than these will they do again: . 1. Ioan. 2.
text.KJV: 14:15 And set aside the thoughts that are most heauy vnto thee, and haste thee to flie from these times.

text.DRB: for look how much the world shall become weak by age, so much shall evils be multiplied upon the inhabitants.
text.KJV: 14:16 For yet greater euils then those which thou hast seene happen, shall bee done hereafter.

text.DRB: For truth hath removed itself farther off, and lying hath approached, for now the vision which thou sawest hasteneth to come.
text.KJV: 14:17 For looke how much the world shall be weaker through age: so much the more shall euils increase vpon them that dwell therein.

text.DRB: And I answered, and said before thee o Lord:
text.KJV: 14:18 For the trueth is fled farre away, and leasing is hard at hand: For now hasteth the vision to come, which thou hast seene.

text.DRB: For behold I will go, as thou hast commanded me, & will rebuke the people that now is. But them that shall yet be born, who shall admonish?
text.KJV: 14:19 Then answered I before thee, and said,

text.DRB: The world therefore is set in darkness, and they that dwell in it without light.
text.KJV: 14:20 Behold, Lord, I will go as thou hast commanded me, and reprooue the people which are present, but they that shall be borne afterward, who shall admonish them? thus the world is set in darkenes, and they that dwell therein, are without light.

text.DRB: Because thy law is burnt, therefore no man knoweth the works that have been done by thee, or that shall begin.
text.KJV: 14:21 For thy law is burnt, therefore no man knoweth the things that are done of thee, or the works that shal begin.

text.DRB: For if I have found grace with thee, send the Holy Ghost to me, & I will write all that hath been done in the world from the beginning, the things that were written in thy law, that men may find the path: and they that will live in the later times, may live.
text.KJV: 14:22 But if I haue found grace before thee, send the holy Ghost into me, and I shall write all that hath bene done in the world, since the beginning, which were written in thy Lawe, that men may find thy path, and that they which will liue in the latter dayes, may liue.

text.DRB: And he answered me, and said: Go gather together the people, and thou shalt say to them, that they seek thee not for forty days.
text.KJV: 14:23 And he answered me, saying, Goe thy way, gather the people together, and say vnto them, that they seeke thee not for fourtie dayes.
14:24 But looke thou prepare thee many boxe trees, and take with thee Sarea, Dabria, Selemia, Eranus and Asiel, these fiue which are ready to write swiftly.
text.NRSV: “But prepare for yourself many writing tablets, and take with you Sarea, Dabria, Selemia, Ethanus, and Asiel—these five, who are trained to write rapidly;”
text.DRB: And do thou prepare thee many tables of box, and take with thee Sarea, Dabria, Salemia, Echanus, and Asiel, these five which are ready to write swiftly.
And come hither, and I will light in thy heart a candle of understanding, which shall not be put out till the things be finished, which thou shalt begin to write.
text.KJV: 14:25 And come hither, and I shall light a candle of vnderstanding in thine heart, which shall not be put out, till the things be performed which thou shalt beginne to write.

text.DRB: And then some things thou shalt open to the perfect, some thou shalt deliver secretly to the wise. For tomorrow this hour thou shalt begin to write.
text.KJV: 14:26 And when thou hast done, some things shalt thou publish, and some things shalt thou shew secretly to the wise: to morrowe this houre shalt thou beginne to write.

text.DRB: And I went as he commanded me, and gathered together all the people, and said:
text.KJV: 14:27 Then went I foorth as he commanded, and gathered all the people together, and said,

text.DRB: Hear Israel these words:
text.KJV: 14:28 Heare these words, O Israel.

text.DRB: Our fathers were pilgrims from the beginning in Ægypt, and were delivered from thence. . . Act. 7.
text.KJV: 14:29 Our fathers at the beginning were strangers in Egypt, from whence they were deliuered:

text.DRB: And they received the law of life, which they kept not, which you also after them have transgressed:
text.KJV: 14:30 And receiued the law of life which they kept not, which ye also haue transgressed after them.

text.DRB: and the land was given you by lot, and the land of Sion, and your fathers, and you have done iniquity, and have not kept the ways which the Highest commanded you.
text.KJV: 14:31 Then was the land, euen the land of Sion, parted among you by lot, but your fathers, and yee your selues haue done vnrighteousnesse, and haue not kept the wayes which the Highest commanded you.

text.DRB: And whereas he is a just judge, he hath taken from you in time that which he had given.
text.KJV: 14:32 And for as much as he is a righteous iudge, hee tooke from you in time, the thing that he had giuen you.

text.DRB: And now you are here, and your brethren are among you.
text.KJV: 14:33 And now are you heere, and your brethren amongst you.

text.DRB: If then you will rule over your sense, & instruct your heart, you shall be preserved alive, and after death shall obtain mercy.
text.KJV: 14:34 Therefore if so be that you will subdue your owne vnderstanding, and reforme your hearts, yee shall be kept aliue, and after death yee shall obtaine mercy.

text.DRB: For the judgement shall come after death, when we shall return to life again: and then the names of the just shall appear, and the deeds of the impious shall be shewed.
text.KJV: 14:35 For after death, shall the iudgement come, when we shall liue againe: and then shall the names of the righteous be manifest, and the workes of the vngodly shall be declared.

text.DRB: Let no man therefore come to me now, nor
text.KJV: 14:36 Let no man therefore come vnto me now, nor seeke after me these fourty dayes.

text.DRB: And I took the five men, as he commanded me, and we went forth into the field, and tarried there.
text.KJV: 14:37 So I tooke the fiue men as hee commanded me, and we went into the field, and remained there.

text.DRB: And I was come to the morrow, and behold a voice called me, saying: Esdras open thy mouth, and drink that which I will give thee to drink. Ezech. 3.
text.KJV: 14:38 And the next day behold a voyce called mee saying, Esdras, open thy mouth and drinke that I giue thee to drinke.

text.DRB: And I opened my mouth, and behold a full cup was brought me, this was full as it were with water: but the colour thereof like as fire.
text.KJV: 14:39 Then opened I my mouth, and behold, he reached me a full cup, which was full as it were with water, but the colour of it was like fire.

text.DRB: And I took it, and drank, and when I had drunken of it, my heart was tormented with understanding, and wisdom grew into my breast. For my spirit was kept by memory.
text.KJV: 14:40 And I tooke it, and dranke: and when I had drunke of it, my heart vttered vnderstanding: and wisedome grew in my brest, for my spirit strengthened my memory.

text.DRB: And my mouth was opened, and was shut no more.
text.KJV: 14:41 And my mouth was opened and shut no more.

text.DRB: The Highest gave understanding unto the five men, and they wrote excesses of the night which were spoken, which they knew not.
text.KJV: 14:42 The highest gaue vnderstanding vnto the fiue men, and they wrote the wonderfull visions of the night, that were told, which they knew not: And they sate fourty dayes, and they wrote in the day, and at night they ate bread.

text.DRB: And at night they did eat bread, but I spake by day, and by night held not my peace.
text.KJV: 14:43 As for me I spake in the day, and held not my tongue by night:
14:44 In fourty dayes they wrote two hundred and foure bookes.
text.NRSV: “In forty days ninety-four books were written.”
text.DRB: And there were written in the forty days two hundred four books.

text.KJV: 14:45 And it came to passe when the fourty dayes were fulfilled, that the Highest spake, saying, The first that thou hast written, publish openly, that the worthy and vnworthy may read it.
text.NRSV: “And when the forty days were ended, the Most High spoke to me, saying, ‘Make public the twenty-four books that you wrote first, and let the worthy and the unworthy read them;’”
text.DRB: And it came to pass when they had ended the forty days, the Highest spake, saying:

text.KJV: 14:46 But keepe the seuenty last, that thou mayest deliuer them onely to such as be wise, among the people.
text.NRSV: “‘but keep the seventy that were written last, in order to give them to the wise among your people.’”
text.DRB: The former things which thou hast written, set abroad, and let the worthy and unworthy read: but the last seventy books thou shalt keep, that thou mayest deliver them to the wise of thy people.
For in these is the vein of understanding, and the fountain of wisdom, and the stream of knowledge. And I did so.
text.KJV: 14:47 For in them is the spring of vnderstanding, the fountains of wisedome, and the streame of knowledge.
15:1 Behold, speake thou in the eares of my people the words of prophesie, which I will put in thy mouth, saith the Lord.
text.NRSV: The Lord says, “Behold, speak in the ears of my people the words of the prophecy that I will put in your mouth,”

text.KJV: 15:2 And cause them to be written in paper: for they are faithfull and true.
text.NRSV: “and cause them to be written on paper; for they are trustworthy and true.”
text.DRB: and see that they be written in paper, because they be faithful and true.
Be not afraid of the cogitations against thee, neither let the incredulities trouble thee of them that speak.
text.KJV: 15:3 Feare not the imaginations against thee, let not the incredulity of them trouble thee, that speake against thee.

text.DRB: Because every incredulous person shall die in his incredulity.
text.KJV: 15:4 For all the vnfaithfull shall die in their vnfaithfulnesse.

text.DRB: Behold I bring in, saith our Lord, upon the whole earth evils, sword, and famine, and death, and destruction.
text.KJV: 15:5 Behold, saith the Lord, I will bring plagues vpon the world; the sword, famine, death, and destruction.

text.DRB: Because iniquity hath fully polluted over all the earth, and their hurtful works are accomplished.
text.KJV: 15:6 For wickednesse hath exceedingly polluted the whole earth, and their hurtfull workes are fulfilled.

text.DRB: Therefore saith our Lord:
text.KJV: 15:7 Therefore saith the Lord,

15:8 I will hold my tongue no more as touching their wickednesse, which they prophanely commit, neither wil I suffer them in those things, in which they wickedly exercise themselues: behold, the innocent & righteous blood cryeth vnto me, and the soules of the iust complaine continually.
text.NRSV: “I will no longer keep silence regarding their impious deeds which they have ungodly committed, nor will I tolerate their wicked practices.”
text.DRB: I will not now keep silence of their impieties which they do irreligiously, neither will I bear with those things, which they practise unjustly. Behold the innocent and just blood cryeth to me, & the souls of the just cry continually.
Revenging I will revenge them, saith our Lord, and I will take all innocent blood out of them unto me. Apoc. 6. v. 10.
text.KJV: 15:9 And therefore saith the Lord, I wil surely auenge them, and receiue vnto me, all the innocent blood from among them.

text.DRB: Behold my people is led to slaughter as a flock, I will no more suffer it to dwell in the land of Ægypt.
text.KJV: 15:10 Beholde, my people is ledde as a flocke to the slaughter: I wil not suffer them now to dwel in the land of Egypt.

text.DRB: But I will bring them forth in a mighty hand and valiant arm, and will strike with plague as before, & will corrupt all the land thereof.
text.KJV: 15:11 But I will bring them with a mighty hand, and a stretched out arme, and smite Egypt with plagues as before, and wil destroy al the land thereof.

text.DRB: Ægypt shall mourn, & the foundations thereof beaten with plague, and with the chastisement which God will bring upon it.
text.KJV: 15:12 Egypt shal mourne, and the foundation of it shall bee smitten with the plague and punishment, that God shall bring vpon it.

text.DRB: The husbandmen that till the ground shall mourn, because their seeds shall perish by blasting, and hail, and by a terrible star.
text.KJV: 15:13 They that till the ground shall mourne: for their seedes shall faile, through the blasting, and haile, and with a fearefull constellation.

text.DRB: Woe to the world and them that dwell therein. . Luc. 21.
text.KJV: 15:14 Woe to the world, and them that dwell therein.

text.DRB: Because the sword is at hand and the destruction of them, and nation shall rise up against nation to fight, and sword in their hands.
text.KJV: 15:15 For the sword and their destruction draweth nigh, and one people shall stand vp to fight against another, and swords in their hands.

text.DRB: For there shall be instability to men, and growing one against another they shall not care for their king, & the Princes of the way of their doings, in their might.
text.KJV: 15:16 For there shalbe sedition among men, and inuading one another, they shal not regard their kings, nor princes, and the course of their actions shall stand in their power.

text.DRB: For a man shall desire to go into the city and cannot.
text.KJV: 15:17 A man shall desire to goe into a citie, and shall not be able.

text.DRB: Because of their prides the cities shall be troubled, the houses raised, the men shall fear.
text.KJV: 15:18 For because of their pride, the cities shalbe troubled, the houses shalbe destroyed, and men shalbe afraid.

text.DRB: Man shall not pity his neighbour, to make their houses nothing worth in the sword, to spoil their goods for famine of bread, & much tribulation.
text.KJV: 15:19 A man shall haue no pitie vpon his neighbour, but shall destroy their houses with the sword, and spoile their goods, because of the lacke of bread, and for great tribulation.

text.DRB: Behold, I call together, saith God, all the kings of the earth to fear me, that are from the Orient, and from the South, from the East, and from
text.KJV: 15:20 Behold, saith God, I will call together all the Kings of the earth to reuerence me, which are from the rising of the Sunne, from the South, from the East, and Libanus: to turne themselues one against another, and repay the things that they haue done to them.

text.DRB: As they do until this day to mine elect, so will I do, and render in their bosom. Thus saith our Lord God:
text.KJV: 15:21 Like as they doe yet this day vnto my chosen, so will I doe also and recompense in their bosome, Thus saith the Lord God;

text.DRB: My right hand shall not spare sinners, neither shall the sword cease upon them that shed innocent blood upon the earth.
text.KJV: 15:22 My right hand shall not spare the sinners, and my sword shal not cease ouer them, that shed innocent blood vpon earth.

text.DRB: Fire came forth from his wrath, and hath devoured the foundations of the earth, and sinners as it were straw set on fire.
text.KJV: 15:23 The fire is gone foorth from his wrath, and hath consumed the foundations of the earth, and the sinners like the straw that is kindled.

text.DRB: Woe to them that sin, and observe not my commandments, saith our Lord.
text.KJV: 15:24 Wo to them that sinne and keepe not my cōmandements, saith the Lord.

text.DRB: I will not spare them: depart o children from the power. Defile not my sanctification:
text.KJV: 15:25 I will not spare them: goe your way ye children from the power, defile not my Sanctuary:

text.DRB: because the Lord knoweth all that sin against him; therefore hath he delivered them into death and into slaughter.
text.KJV: 15:26 For the Lord knoweth all them that sinne against him, and therefore deliuereth he them vnto death and destruction.

text.DRB: For now are evils come upon the world, and you shall tarry in them. For God will not deliver you, because you have sinned against him.
text.KJV: 15:27 For now are the plagues come vpon the whole earth, and ye shall remaine in them, for God shal not deliuer you, because ye haue sinned against him.

text.DRB: Behold an horrible vision, and the face of it from the east.
text.KJV: 15:28 Behold an horrible vision, and the appearance thereof from the East.

text.DRB: And the nations of dragons of Arabians shall come forth in many chariots, and as a wind the number of them is carried upon the earth, so that now all do fear and tremble, that shall hear them.
text.KJV: 15:29 Where the nations of the dragons of Arabia shall come out with many charets, and the multitude of them shalbe caried as the winde vpon earth, that all they which heare them, may feare and tremble.

text.DRB: The Carmonians mad for anger, and they shall go forth as wild boars out of the wood, and they shall come with great power, and shall stand in fight with them, and they shall waste the portion of the land of the Assyrians.
text.KJV: 15:30 Also the Carmanians raging in wrath, shall go forth as the wilde bores of the wood, and with great power shall they come, and ioyne battell with them, and shall waste a portion of the land of the Assyrians.

text.DRB: And after these things the dragons shall prevail mindful of their nativity, & conspiring shall turn themselves in great force to pursue them.
text.KJV: 15:31 And then shall the dragons haue the vpper hand, remembring their nature, and if they shall turne themselues, conspiring together in great power to persecute them,

text.DRB: These shall be troubled and hold their peace at their force, and shall turn their feet into flight.
text.KJV: 15:32 Then these shalbe troubled, and keepe silence through their power, and shall flee.

text.DRB: And from the territory of the Assyrians the besiegers shall besiege them, and shall consume one of them, and there shall be fear and trembling in their army, and contention against their kings.
text.KJV: 15:33 And from the land of the Assyrians, shall the enemy besiege them, and consume some of them, and in their host shall be feare, and dread and strife among their kings.

text.DRB: Behold clouds from the east, and from the north unto the south, and their face very horrible, full of wrath and storm.
text.KJV: 15:34 Behold clouds from the East, and from the North, vnto the South, and they are very horrible to looke vpon; full of wrath and storme.

text.DRB: And they shall beat one against another, & they shall beat down many stars, and their star upon the earth, and blood shall be from the sword unto the belly.
text.KJV: 15:35 They shall smite one vpon another, & they shall smite downe a great multitude of starres vpon the earth, euen their owne starre; and blood shalbe from the sword vnto the belly.

text.DRB: And man's dung unto the camel's litter, and there shall be much fear, and trembling upon the earth.
text.KJV: 15:36 And doung of men vnto the camels hough.

text.DRB: And they shall shake that shall see that wrath, & tremble shall take them: and after these things there shall many showers be moved:
text.KJV: 15:37 And there shalbe great fearefulnesse and trembling vpon earth: and they that see the wrath, shall be afraid, and trembling shall come vpon them.

text.DRB: from the south, and the north: and another portion from the west.
text.KJV: 15:38 And then shall there come great stormes, from the South, and from the North, & another part from the West.

text.DRB: And the winds from the east shall prevail upon it, and shall shut it up, and the clouds which he raised in wrath, and the star to make terror to the east wind, and the west shall be destroyed.
text.KJV: 15:39 And strong winds shal arise from the East, and shall open it, and the cloud which hee raised vp in wrath, and the starre stirred to cause feare toward the East and West winde, shalbe destroyed.

text.DRB: And there shall be exalted great and mighty clouds full of wrath, and a star to terrify all the earth, and the inhabitants thereof, and they shall pour in upon every high and eminent place a terrible star,
text.KJV: 15:40 The great and mightie cloudes shall be lifted vp full of wrath, and the starre, that they may make all the earth afraid, and them that dwel therein, and they shall powre out ouer euery high and eminent place, an horrible starre.

text.DRB: fire, and hail, & flying swords, and many waters, so that all fields also shall be filled, and all rivers with the fullness of many waters.
text.KJV: 15:41 Fire and haile, and fleeing swords, and many waters, that all fields may be full, and all riuers with the abundance of great waters.

text.DRB: And they shall throw down cities, and walls, and mountains, and hills, and the trees of the woods, and the grass of the meadows, and their corn.
text.KJV: 15:42 And they shal breake downe the cities, and walls, mountaines and hils, trees of the wood, and grasse of the medowes, and their corne.

text.DRB: And they shall pass constant unto Babylon, and shall raise her.
text.KJV: 15:43 And they shal goe stedfastly vnto Babylon, and make her afraid.

text.DRB: They shall come together against her, and shall compass her, and shall pour out the star, and all wrath upon her, and the dust and smoke shall go up even into heaven, and round about shall lament her. Apoc. 18.
text.KJV: 15:44 They shall come to her, and besiege her, the starre and all wrath shall they powre out vpon her, then shall the dust and smoke goe vp vnto the heauen: and all they that be about her, shall bewaile her.

text.DRB: And they that shall remain under her, shall serve them that terrified her.
text.KJV: 15:45 And they that remaine vnder her, shall doe seruice vnto them that haue put her in feare.

text.DRB: And thou Asia agreeing into the hope of Babylon, and the glory of her person,
text.KJV: 15:46 And thou Asia that art partaker of the hope of Babylon, and art the glory of her person:

text.DRB: woe be to thee thou wretch, because thou art like to her, and hast adorned thy daughters in fornication, to please & glory in thy lovers, which have desired always to fornicate with thee.
text.KJV: 15:47 Woe be vnto thee thou wretch, because thou hast made thy selfe like vnto her, and hast deckt thy daughters in whoredome, that they might please and glory in thy louers, which haue alway desired to commit whordome with thee.

text.DRB: Thou hast imitated the odious in all her works, and in her inventions: therefore saith God:
text.KJV: 15:48 Thou hast followed her, that is hated in all her works and inuentions: therefore sayth God,

text.DRB: I will send in evils upon thee, widowhood, poverty, and famine, and sword, and pestilence, to destroy thy houses by violation, and death, and glory of thy virtue.
text.KJV: 15:49 I will send plagues vpon thee: widowhood, pouertie, famine, sword, and pestilence, to waste thy houses with destruction and death.

text.DRB: As a flower shall be withered, when the heat shall rise that is sent forth upon thee,
text.KJV: 15:50 And the glory of the power shall be dried vp as floure, when the heate shall arise that is sent ouer thee.

text.DRB: thou shalt be weakened as a little poor soul plagued and chastised of women, that the mighty and the lovers may not receive thee.
text.KJV: 15:51 Thou shalt bee weakened as a poore woman with stripes, and as one chastised with woundes, so that the mightie and louers shall not be able to receiue thee.

text.DRB: Will I be zealous against thee saith our Lord,
text.KJV: 15:52 Would I with iealousie haue so proceeded against thee, saith the Lord,

text.DRB: unless thou hadst slain mine elect at all times, exalting the slaughter of the hands, and saying upon their death, when thou was drunken.
text.KJV: 15:53 If thou haddest not alway slaine my chosen, exalting the stroke of thine hands, & saying ouer their dead, when thou wast drunken,

text.DRB: Adorn the beauty of thy countenance.
text.KJV: 15:54 Set foorth the beauty of thy countenance.

text.DRB: The reward of thy fornication is in thy bosom, therefore thou shalt receive recompence.
text.KJV: 15:55 The reward of thy whoredome shall be in thy bosome, therefore shalt thou receiue recompense.

text.DRB: And thy children shall die for famine: and thou shalt fall by the sword, and thy cities shall be destroyed, and all thine shall fall in the field by the sword.
text.KJV: 15:57 Thy children shall die of hunger, and thou shalt fall through the sword: thy cities shalbe broken downe, and all thine shall perish with the sword in the field.

text.DRB: And they that are in the mountains shall perish with famine, and shall eat their own flesh, and drink blood, for the famine of bread and thirst of waters.
text.KJV: 15:58 They that be in the mountaines shall die of hunger, and eate their owne flesh, and drinke their owne blood, for very hunger of bread, & thirst of water.

text.DRB: Unhappy by the seas shalt thou come, and again thou shalt receive evils.
text.KJV: 15:59 Thou, as vnhappy, shalt come through the Sea, and receiue plagues againe.

text.DRB: And in the passage they shall beat against the idle city, and shall destroy some portion of thy land, and shall deface part of thy glory, again returning to Babylon overthrown.
text.KJV: 15:60 And in the passage, they shall rush on the idle citie, and shall destroy some portion of thy land, and consume part of thy glory, and shall returne to Babylon that was destroyed.

text.DRB: And being thrown down thou shalt be to them for stubble, and they shall be to thee fire:
text.KJV: 15:61 And thou shalt be cast downe by them, as stubble, and they shall be vnto thee as fire,

text.DRB: and devour thee, and thy cities, thy land, and thy mountains, all thy woods and fruitful trees they will burn with fire.
text.KJV: 15:62 And shall consume thee and thy cities, thy land and thy mountaines, all thy woods and thy fruitfull trees shall they burne vp with fire.

text.DRB: Thy children they shall lead captive, and shall have thy goods for a prey, and the glory of thy face they shall destroy.
text.KJV: 15:63 Thy children shall they cary away captiue, and looke what thou hast, they shall spoile it, and marre the beauty of thy face.
16:1 Woe be vnto thee, Babylon and Asia, woe be vnto thee Egypt and Syria.
text.NRSV: Woe to you, Babylon and Asia! Woe to you, Egypt and Syria!
text.DRB: Table OF
Gird yourselves with sackcloths and shirts of hair, and mourn for your children, and be sorry: because your destruction is at hand.
text.KJV: 16:2 Gird vp your selues with clothes of sacke and haire, bewaile your children, and be sory, for your destruction is at hand.

text.DRB: The sword is sent in upon you, and who is he that can turn it away?
text.KJV: 16:3 A sword is sent vpon you, and who may turne it backe?

text.DRB: Fire is sent in upon you, and who is he that can quench it?
text.KJV: 16:4 A fire is sent among you, and who may quench it?

text.DRB: Evils are sent in upon you, and who is he that can repel them?
text.KJV: 16:5 Plagues are sent vnto you, and what is he that may driue them away?

text.DRB: Shall any man repel the lion being hungry in the wood, or quench the fire in stubble, forthwith when it beginneth to burn?
text.KJV: 16:6 May any man driue away a hungry Lion in the wood? or may any one quench the fire in stubble, when it hath begun to burne?

text.DRB: Shall any man repel the arrow shot of a strong archer?
text.KJV: 16:7 May one turne againe the arrow that is shot of a strong archer?

text.DRB: Our strong Lord sendeth in evils, & who is he that can repel them?
text.KJV: 16:8 The mightie Lord sendeth the plagues, and who is hee that can driue them away?

text.DRB: Fire came forth from his wrath, and who is he that can quench it?
text.KJV: 16:9 A fire shall goe foorth from his wrath: & who is he that may quench it?

text.DRB: He will lighten, who shall not fear, he will thunder, and who shall not be afraid?
text.KJV: 16:10 He shall cast lightnings, and who shall not feare? he shall thunder, and who shall not be afraid?

text.DRB: Our Lord will threaten, and who shall not utterly be destroyed before his face?
text.KJV: 16:11 The Lord shall threaten, and who shall not be vtterly beaten to powder at his presence?

text.DRB: The earth hath trembled, and the foundations thereof, the sea tosseth up waves from the depth, and the floods of it shall be destroyed, and the fishes thereof at the face of our Lord, and at the glory of his power:
text.KJV: 16:12 The earth quaketh and the foundations thereof, the sea ariseth vp with waues from the deepe, and the waues of it are troubled, and the fishes thereof also before the Lord, and before the glorie of his power.

text.DRB: because his right hand is strong which bendeth the bow, his arrows be sharp that are shot of him, they shall not miss, when they shall begin to be shot into the ends of the earth.
text.KJV: 16:13 For strong is his right hand that bendeth the bow, his arrowes that hee shooteth are sharpe, and shall not misse when they begin to bee shot into the ends of the world.

text.DRB: Behold evils are sent, and they shall not return till they come upon the earth.
text.KJV: 16:14 Behold, the plagues are sent, and shall not returne againe, vntill they come vpon the earth.

text.DRB: The fire is kindled and it shall not be quenched, till it consume the foundations of the earth.
text.KJV: 16:15 The fire is kindled, and shall not be put out, till it consume the foundation of the earth.

text.DRB: For as the arrow shot of a strong archer returneth not, so shall not the evils return back, that shall be sent upon the earth.
text.KJV: 16:16 Like as an arrow which is shot of a mightie archer returneth not backward: euen so the plagues that shall be sent vpon earth, shall not returne againe.

text.DRB: Woe is me: woe is me: who shall deliver me in those days?
text.KJV: 16:17 Woe is me, woe is me, who will deliuer me in those dayes?

text.DRB: The beginning of sorrows and much mourning, the beginning of famine and much destruction. The beginning of wars and the potestates shall fear, the beginning of evils and all shall tremble.
text.KJV: 16:18 The beginning of sorrowes, and great mournings, the beginning of famine, and great death: the beginning of warres, and the powers shall stand in feare, the beginning of euils, what shall I doe when these euils shal come?

text.DRB: In these what shall I do, when the evils shall come?
text.KJV: 16:19 Behold, famine, and plague, tribulation and anguish, are sent as scourges for amendment.

text.DRB: Behold famine, and plague, & tribulation, and distress are sent all as scourges for amendment,
text.KJV: 16:20 But for all these things they shall not turne from their wickednes, nor be alway mindfull of the scourges.

text.DRB: and in all these they will not convert themselves from their iniquities, neither will they be always mindful of the scourges.
text.KJV: 16:21 Behold, victuals shall be so good cheape vpon earth, that they shal think themselues to be in good case, and euen then shall euils growe vpon earth, sword, famine, and great confusion.

text.DRB: Behold, there shall be good cheap victuals upon the earth, so that they may think that peace is directly coming toward them, & then shall evils spring upon the earth, sword, famine, and great confusion.
text.KJV: 16:22 For many of them that dwell vpon earth, shall perish of famine, and the other that escape the hunger, shall the sword destroy.

text.DRB: For by famine many that inhabit the earth shall die, and the sword shall destroy the rest that remained alive of the famine,
text.KJV: 16:23 And the dead shall be cast out as doung, and there shalbe no man to comfort them, for the earth shall be wasted, and the cities shall be cast downe.

text.DRB: and the dead shall be cast forth as dung, and there shall be none to comfort them. For the earth shall be left desert, and the cities thereof shall be thrown down.
text.KJV: 16:24 There shall be no man left to till the earth, and to sow it.

text.DRB: There shall not be left a man to till the ground and to sow it.
text.KJV: 16:25 The trees shall giue fruite, and who shall gather them?

text.DRB: The trees shall yield fruits, and who shall gather them?
text.KJV: 16:26 The grapes shall ripe, and who shall treade them? for all places shall be desolate of men.

text.DRB: The grape shall become ripe, and who shall tread it? For there shall be great desolation to
text.KJV: 16:27 So that one man shall desire to see another, and to heare his voyce.

text.DRB: For a man shall desire to see a man, or to hear his voice.
text.KJV: 16:28 For of a citie there shalbe ten left, and two of the field which shall hide themselues in the thicke groues, and in the clefts of rockes.

text.DRB: For there shall be left ten of a city, and two of the field that have hid themselves in thick woods, and cliffs of rocks.
text.KJV: 16:29 As in an orchard of oliues, vpon euery tree there are left three or foure oliues:

text.DRB: As there are left in the olivet, and on every tree, three or four olives.
text.KJV: 16:30 Or, when as a vineyard is gathered, there are left some clusters of them that diligently seek through þ vineyard:

text.DRB: Or as in a vineyard when it is gathered there are grapes left by them, that diligently search the vineyard:
text.KJV: 16:31 Euen so in those dayes there shalbe three or foure left by them that search their houses with the sword.

text.DRB: so shall there be left in those days three or four, by them that search their houses in the sword.
text.KJV: 16:32 And the earth shall be laid waste, and the fields therof shal waxe old, and her wayes and all her paths shall grow full of thornes, because no man shal trauaile therethrough.

text.DRB: And the earth shall be left desolate, and the fields thereof shall wax old, and the ways thereof, and all the paths thereof shall bring forth thorns, because no man shall pass by it.
text.KJV: 16:33 The virgins shall mourne hauing no bridegromes, þ women shal mourne hauing no husbands, their daughters shall mourne hauing no helpers.

text.DRB: Virgins shall mourn having no bridegrooms, women shall mourn having no husbands, their daughters shall mourn having no help:
text.KJV: 16:34 In the warres shall their bridegromes bee destroyed, and their husbands shall perish of famine.

text.DRB: Their bridegrooms shall be consumed in battle, and their husbands be destroyed in famine.
text.KJV: 16:35 Heare now these things, and vnderstand them, ye seruants of the Lord.

text.DRB: But hear these things, and know them ye servants of our Lord.
text.KJV: 16:36 Behold the word of the Lord, receiue it, beleeue not the gods of whom the Lord spake.

text.DRB: Behold the word of our Lord, receive it: believe not the gods of whom our Lord speaketh.
text.KJV: 16:37 Behold, the plagues draw nigh, and are not slacke.

text.DRB: Behold the evils approach, and slack not.
text.KJV: 16:38 As when a woman with childe in the ninth month bringeth forth her son, within two or three houres of her birth great paines compasse her wombe, which paines, when the child commeth forth, they slacke not a moment,

text.DRB: As a woman with child when she bringeth forth her child in the ninth month, the hour of her deliverance approaching, two or three hours before, pains come about her womb, and the infants coming out of her womb, they will not tarry one moment.
text.KJV: 16:39 Euen so shall not the plagues bee slacke to come vpon the earth, and the world shall mourne, and sorrowes shal come vpon it on euery side.

text.DRB: So the evils shall not slack to come forth upon the earth, and the world shall lament, and sorrows shall hold it round about.
text.KJV: 16:40 O my people, Heare my word: make you ready to the battell, and in those euils, be euen as pilgrimes vpon the earth.

text.DRB: Hear the word, my people: prepare yourselves unto the fight, & in the evils so be ye as strangers of the earth.
text.KJV: 16:41 He that selleth let him be as hee that fleeth away: and he that buyeth, as one that will loose.

text.DRB: He that selleth as if he should fly, & he that buyeth as he that should lose it.
text.KJV: 16:42 He that occupieth merchandize, as he that had no profit by it: and he that buildeth, as hee that shall not dwell therein.

text.DRB: He that playeth the merchant, as he that should take no fruit: and he that buildeth as he that should not inhabit.
text.KJV: 16:43 He that soweth, as if he should not reape: so also he that planteth the vineyard, as he that shal not gather the grapes.

text.DRB: He that soweth, as he that shall not reap: so he also that pruneth a vineyard, as if he should not have the vintage.
text.KJV: 16:44 They that marry, as they that shall get no children: and they that marrie not, as the widowers.

text.DRB: They that marry so as if they should not get children, and they that marry not, so as it were widows.
text.KJV: 16:45 And therefore they that labour, labour in vaine.

text.DRB: Wherefore they that labour, labour without cause:
text.KJV: 16:46 For strangers shall reape their fruits, and spoile their goods, ouerthrowe their houses; and take their children captiues, for in captiuity and famine shall they get children.

text.DRB: for foreigners shall reap their fruits, and shall violently take their goods, and overthrow their houses, and lead their children captive, because in captivity, and famine they beget their children.
text.KJV: 16:47 And they that occupy their merchandize with robbery, the more they decke their citties, their houses, their possessions and their owne persons:

text.DRB: And they that play the merchants by robbery, the longer they adorn their cities and houses, and their possessions and persons:
text.KJV: 16:48 The more will I be angry with them for their sinne, saith the Lord.

text.DRB: so much the more will I be zealous toward them, upon their sins, saith our Lord.
text.KJV: 16:49 Like as an whore enuieth a right honest and vertuous woman:

text.DRB: As a whore envieth an honest and very good woman:
text.KJV: 16:50 So shall righteousnesse haue iniquity, when she decketh herselfe, and shall accuse her, to her face, when he commeth that shall defend him that diligently searcheth out euery sinne vpon earth.

text.DRB: so shall justice hate impiety when she adorneth herself, and accuseth her to her face, when he shall come that may defend him that searcheth out all upon the earth.
text.KJV: 16:51 And therfore be yee not like therunto, nor to the workes thereof.

text.DRB: Therefore be not made like to her, nor to her works.
text.KJV: 16:52 For yet a little iniquitie shall be taken away out of the earth, and righteousnesse shall reigne among you.

text.DRB: For yet a little while and iniquity shall be taken away from the earth, and justice shall reign over you.
text.KJV: 16:53 Let not the sinner say that he hath not sinned: for God shall burne coales of fire vpon his head, which saith before the Lord God and his glory, I haue not sinned.

text.DRB: Let not the sinner say he hath not sinned: because he shall burn coals of fire upon his head, that saith I have not sinned before our Lord God and his glory.
text.KJV: 16:54 Behold, the Lord knoweth all the workes of men, their imaginations, their thoughts, and their hearts:

text.DRB: Behold our Lord shall know all the works of men, and their inventions, and their cogitations, and their hearts. . Luc. 16. .
text.KJV: 16:55 Which spake but the word, let the earth be made, and it was made: let the heauen be made, and it was created.

text.DRB: For he said: Let the earth be made, and it was made: let the heaven be made, and it was made.
text.KJV: 16:56 In his word were the starres made, and he knoweth the number of them.

text.DRB: And by his word the stars were made, and he knoweth the number of the stars. . v. 4. Job. 38.
text.KJV: 16:57 He searcheth the deepe, and the treasures thereof, he hath measured the Sea, and what it containeth.

text.DRB: Who searcheth the depth and the treasures thereof: who hath measured the sea, and capacity thereof.
text.KJV: 16:58 He hath shut the Sea in the midst of the waters, and with his word hath he hanged the earth vpon the waters.

text.DRB: Who hath shut up the sea in the midst of waters, and hath hanged the earth upon the waters with his word.
text.KJV: 16:59 He spreadeth out the heauens like a vault, vpon the waters hath he founded it.

text.DRB: Who hath spread heaven as it were a vault, over the waters he hath founded it.
text.KJV: 16:60 In the desart hath hee made springs of water, and pooles vpon the tops of the mountaines, that the floods might powre downe from the high rockes to water the earth.

text.DRB: Who hath put fountains of waters in the desert, and lakes upon the tops of mountains, to send forth rivers from the high rock to water the earth.
text.KJV: 16:61 He made man, and put his heart in the midst of the body, and gaue him breath, life, and vnderstanding.

text.DRB: Who made man and put his heart in the midst of the body, and gave him spirit, life, and understanding.
text.KJV: 16:62 Yea and the spirit of Almighty God, which made all things, and searcheth out all hidden things in the secrets of the earth.

text.DRB: And the inspiration of God omnipotent that made all things, and searcheth all hid things, in the secrets of the earth.
text.KJV: 16:63 Surely he knoweth your inuentions, and what you thinke in your hearts, euen them that sinne, and would hide their sinne.

text.DRB: He knoweth your invention, and what you think in your hearts sinning, and willing to hide your sins.
text.KJV: 16:64 Therefore hath the Lord exactly searched out all your workes, and he will put you all to shame.

text.DRB: Wherefore our Lord in searching hath searched all your works, and he will put you all to open shame,
text.KJV: 16:65 And when your sinnes are brought foorth yee shalbe ashamed before men, and your owne sinnes shall be your accusers in that day.

text.DRB: and you shall be confounded when your sins shall
text.KJV: 16:66 What will yee doe? or how will yee hide your sinnes before God and his Angels?

16:67 Behold, God himselfe is the iudge, feare him: leaue off from your sinnes, and forget your iniquities to medle no more with them for euer, so shall God lead you forth, and deliuer you from all trouble.
text.NRSV: Behold, God is the judge; fear him! Do not sin any more, and forget your iniquities, to never commit them again; so God will lead you forth and deliver you from all tribulation.
text.DRB: What will you do? Or how shall you hide your sins before God and his Angels?
Behold God is the Judge, fear him. Cease from your sins, and now forget your iniquities to do them any more, and God will bring you out, and deliver you from all tribulation.
text.KJV: 16:68 For behold, the burning wrath of a great multitude is kindled ouer you, and they shall take away certaine of you, and feede you being idle with things offered vnto idoles.

text.DRB: For behold the heat of a great multitude is kindled over you, and they shall take certain of you by violence, and shall make the slain to be meat for idols.
text.KJV: 16:69 And they that consent vnto them shall be had in derision, and in reproch, and troden vnder foote.

text.DRB: And they that shall consent unto them, shall be to them in derision, and in reproach, and in conculcation.
text.KJV: 16:70 For there shall be in euery place, and in the next cities a great insurrection vpon those that feare the Lord.

text.DRB: for there shall be place against places, and against the next cities great insurrection upon them that fear our Lord.
text.KJV: 16:71 They shall be like mad men, sparing none, but still spoiling and destroying those that feare the Lord.

text.DRB: They shall be as it were mad sparing nobody, to spoil and waste yet them that fear our Lord.
text.KJV: 16:72 For they shal waste and take away their goods, and cast them out of their houses.

text.DRB: because they shall waste and spoil the goods, and shall cast them out of their houses.
text.KJV: 16:73 Then shall they be knowen who are my chosen, and they shall be tried, as the gold in the fire:

text.DRB: Then, shall appear the probation of mine elect, as gold that is proved by the fire.
text.KJV: 16:74 Heare, O yee my beloued, saith the Lord: behold, the dayes of trouble are at hand, but I will deliuer you from the same.

text.DRB: Hear my beloved, saith our Lord: Behold the days of tribulation are come: and out of them I will deliver you.
text.KJV: 16:75 Be yee not afraid, neither doubt, for God is your guide,

text.DRB: Do not fear, nor stagger, because God is your guide.
text.KJV: 16:76 And the guide of them who keepe my commaundements, and precepts, saith the Lord God; Let not your sinnes weigh you downe, and let not your iniquities lift vp themselues.

text.DRB: And he that keepeth my commandments, and precepts, saith our Lord God: Let not your sins overweigh you, nor your iniquities be advanced over you.
text.KJV: 16:77 Woe bee vnto them that are bound with their sinnes, and couered with their iniquities: like as a field is couered ouer with bushes, and the path thereof couered with thornes, that no man may trauell through.
16:78 It is left vndressed, and is cast into the fire, to bee consumed therewith.
text.NRSV: Woe to those who are bound by their sins and overwhelmed by their iniquities, as a field which is choked with undergrowth and its path overwhelmed with thorns, so that no one can pass through! It is shut off and given up to be consumed by fire.
text.DRB: Woe to them that are entangled with their sins, and are covered with their iniquities, as a field is entangled with the wood, and the path thereof covered with thorns, by which no man passeth, and it is closed out, and cast to be devoured of the fire.
